fab_section_md:
- Lord of the Eyrie and Protector of the Vale, a lord since the age of ten who served twenty years on the small council as lord justiciar and master of laws (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:17)
- At six-and-thirty he was twenty years older than Princess Daella, short and balding, and a widower with four children by his late first wife. Princess Daella chose him as husband (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:25)
- — and they wed at Dragonstone before year's end, with no bedding at her wish. He was a kind husband who called her "my precious princess," and was later replaced as justiciar and master of laws by Prince Aemon (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:39)
identity_line: Rodrik Arryn — Lord of the Eyrie and Protector of the Vale, a lord since the age of ten who served twenty years on the small council as lord justiciar and master of laws.

fab_section_md:
- Daughter of King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne, so delicate she feared crowds and cats and dreaded a bedding. She wed Lord Rodrik Arryn and went to the Vale, clashing at once with his eldest daughter Elys but doting on the younger children. She bore a daughter, Aemma, a fortnight early after a long and troubled labor (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:59)
- Childbed fever set in and she died at eighteen in 82 AC (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:63)
- — and Alysanne had her burned and her ashes interred on Dragonstone (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:65)

- King and husband of Queen Alysanne. He named Prince Aemon justiciar and master of laws in Rodrik Arryn's place, and led his sons Aemon and Baelon with dragons to destroy Morion Martell's fleet, having long known of the attack through spies (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:81)
- In the Saera affair he sat in judgment from the Iron Throne, imprisoned her three lords, and after her taunts disowned her (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:231)
identity_line: Jaehaerys I Targaryen — King and husband of Queen Alysanne. He named Prince Aemon justiciar and master of laws in Rodrik Arryn's place, and led his sons Aemon and Baelon with dragons to destroy Morion Martell's fleet, having long known of the attack through spies.

fab_section_md:
- The ninthborn child of King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne, born 67 AC, willful and voracious where her sister Daella was timid (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:105)
- As a girl she tormented Daella with cats and bees, dyed the Kingsguard's white cloaks pink, stole wine, and used the fool Tom Turnip as her catspaw. She took three young lords — Jonah Mooton, Red Roy Connington, and Stinger Beesbury — as lovers and confessed she had given her maidenhead to all three (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:215)
- When confined she escaped in a washerwoman's robes toward the Dragonpit and was taken and confined to a tower cell (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:243)
identity_line: Saera Targaryen — The ninthborn child of King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne, born 67 AC, willful and voracious where her sister Daella was timid.

fab_section_md:
- Prince and heir apparent to the Iron Throne, who reached his twenty-sixth nameday in 81 AC and had proved able in war and peace (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:37)
- King Jaehaerys named him justiciar and master of laws in place of Rodrik Arryn (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:39)
identity_line: Aemon Targaryen — Prince and heir apparent to the Iron Throne, who reached his twenty-sixth nameday in 81 AC and had proved able in war and peace.

fab_section_md:
- Princess and wife of Prince Baelon, irrepressible and bold, "with the broken nose and mismatched eyes." She bore Daemon in 81 AC and flew him aloft on Meleys within a fortnight (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:43)
- She bore a third son, Aegon, in 84 AC after a long labor, never fully recovered, and died within the year at four-and-twenty (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:99)
identity_line: Alyssa Targaryen — Princess and wife of Prince Baelon, irrepressible and bold, "with the broken nose and mismatched eyes." She bore Daemon in 81 AC and flew him aloft on Meleys within a fortnight.

fab_section_md: - Daughter of Lord Rodrik Arryn and Princess Daella, born a fortnight early in 82 AC after a troubled labor (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:59)
identity_line: Aemma Arryn — Daughter of Lord Rodrik Arryn and Princess Daella, born a fortnight early in 82 AC after a troubled labor.

fab_section_md: - Prince of Dorne who succeeded his father in Sunspear, rash and foolish, resentful of Dornish inaction during Lord Rogar's War (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:73)
identity_line: Morion Martell — Prince of Dorne who succeeded his father in Sunspear, rash and foolish, resentful of Dornish inaction during Lord Rogar's War.

fab_section_md: - A nineteen-year-old knight called "Stinger," the finest lance in the Reach and heir to Honeyholt, one of Saera's three favorites (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:127)
identity_line: Braxton Beesbury — A nineteen-year-old knight called "Stinger," the finest lance in the Reach and heir to Honeyholt, one of Saera's three favorites.

fab_section_md:
- A maid of Saera's own age called "Sweetberry," one of Saera's dearest friends (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:127)
- She confessed the truth to Queen Alysanne and revealed she was with child by an unknown father (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:173)
identity_line: Alys Turnberry — A maid of Saera's own age called "Sweetberry," one of Saera's dearest friends.

fab_section_md: - A maid of Saera's own age called "Pretty Peri," one of Saera's dearest friends, who confessed the whole affair to Queen Alysanne.
identity_line: Perianne Moore — A maid of Saera's own age called "Pretty Peri," one of Saera's dearest friends, who confessed the whole affair to Queen Alysanne.

fab_section_md: - The king's half-witted fool, victim and unwitting catspaw of Saera's japes; she once had him climb the Iron Throne, which cut his arms and legs, and later had him brought naked to the Blue Pearl (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:117)
identity_line: Tom Turnip — The king's half-witted fool, victim and unwitting catspaw of Saera's japes; she once had him climb the Iron Throne, which cut his arms and legs, and later had him brought naked to the Blue Pearl.

fab_section_md: - Eldest daughter of Lord Rodrik, three years older than her stepmother Daella, with whom she clashed from the first (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:33)
identity_line: Elys Arryn — Eldest daughter of Lord Rodrik, three years older than her stepmother Daella, with whom she clashed from the first.

fab_section_md: - Lord Rodrik's younger daughter, who wrote most of Daella's letters home (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:33)
identity_line: Amanda Arryn — Lord Rodrik's younger daughter, who wrote most of Daella's letters home.

fab_section_md: - Ser Robert Redwyne, commander of the City Watch, who delivered the three lords to the king and ignored their threats and bribery (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:139)
identity_line: Robert Redwyne — Ser Robert Redwyne, commander of the City Watch, who delivered the three lords to the king and ignored their threats and bribery.

fab_section_md: - Became the Princess of Dorne after the Fourth Dornish War (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:87)
identity_line: Mara Martell — Became the Princess of Dorne after the Fourth Dornish War.

fab_section_md: - Lord of Storm's End, made aware of Morion's coming and waiting on Cape Wrath to meet the Dornishmen (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p03:85)
identity_line: Boremund Baratheon — Lord of Storm's End, made aware of Morion's coming and waiting on Cape Wrath to meet the Dornishmen.
